avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Above approximately 1800 m the wind slabs are larger. Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong northeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form in the late morning in particular in places that are protected from the wind. In all aspects medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible. In particular in the regions with a lot of snow the avalanches can penetrate even deep layers.

snowpackStructure.comment: A lot of snow has fallen over a wide area. In some places new snow and wind slabs are lying on a moist old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
20 to 40 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1600 m. Since Monday the wind has been strong.

The clearly visible wind slabs of the last few days can be released by a single winter sport participant in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above the tree line. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be triggered in the various wind slab layers and reach large size. In particular places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are unfavourable.

Caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of a strong northeasterly wind, sometimes deep wind slabs formed since Monday in particular in areas not adjacent to ridgelines.
In addition soft wind slabs formed in places that are protected from the wind.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was good. Low and intermediate altitudes sunny slopes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust.

Adjacent to ridgelines in high Alpine regions snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force wind, clearly visible wind slabs formed in all aspects. As a consequence of a sometimes strong northeasterly wind, further wind slabs will form on Wednesday. These can be released easily above the tree line. Caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized. Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

Loose snow avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small. In the event of solar radiation this applies in particular, especially on extremely steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last few days. The sometimes storm force wind has transported the new snow. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form in all aspects. The old wind slabs will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to softening of the snowpack below approximately 2000 m. The more recent wind slabs represent the main danger. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, sometimes large wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in all aspects and generally above the tree line.
The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to softening of the snowpack below approximately 2000 m.

tendency.highlights: Friday: The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1600 m. Since Monday the wind has been strong.

The clearly visible wind slabs of the last few days can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases in particular on west to north to east facing aspects at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can be triggered in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size. In particular places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are unfavourable.

Caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of a strong northeasterly wind, clearly visible wind slabs formed since Monday in all aspects.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was good. In particular low and intermediate altitudes sunny slopes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust.

Adjacent to ridgelines in high Alpine regions snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moderate avalanche danger will prevail. At elevated altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment: The wind slabs represent the main danger. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can still be released. This applies in particular in case of a large load. Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack's surface remains generally characterized by alternating eroded areas interspersed with deposited zones; in many areas, hard surface crusts are present, locally hidden. Near the crests, evident cornices remain in many places. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be sunny at times. The wind will be light to moderate.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the sometimes strong wind the wind slabs have increased in size moderately. These can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.
Especially on wind-loaded slopes the dry avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size in isolated cases.
In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 2000 m. Until Wednesday the wind will be strong.
In some localities 2 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: As you gain altitude, snow cover decreases and becomes uneven. Snow cover is often better at intermediate altitudes, where it's less affected by the wind.
The snow on the surface changes rapidly depending on the exposure and slope: crusts of various nature and thickness often alternate.
In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
The solar radiation gave rise to settling of the snowpack.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather conditions will facilitate a strengthening of the snow drift accumulations. The avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from northerly directions, visible wind slabs formed also in areas not adjacent to ridgelines. These can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases. In particular along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland and at intermediate and high altitudes these avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is greater, caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. Especially here the dry avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.
Where the wind was less intense, soft slabs have formed. They are bonding with the old snowpack, but are currently hardly detectable. Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.
As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the natural avalanche activity will slowly increase, in particular on extremely steep sunny slopes. As the day progresses, individual moist snow slides are possible. This also applies at the base of rock walls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities 10 to 20 cm of snow fell yesterday above approximately 2000 m. Until Wednesday the wind will be strong adjacent to ridgelines.
5 to 20 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: As you gain altitude, snow cover decreases and becomes uneven. Snow cover is often better at intermediate altitudes, where it's less affected by the wind.
The snow on the surface changes rapidly depending on the exposure and slope: crusts of various nature and thickness often alternate. It is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases.
In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface, in particular in places that are protected from the wind.
The solar radiation gave rise to settling of the snowpack.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather conditions will facilitate a strengthening of the snow drift accumulations. The avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Some snow has fallen since Sunday in some regions. Since Monday the wind has been strong. The clearly visible wind slabs of the last few days can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases at intermediate and high altitudes, especially at their margins.

Places where persistent weak layers exist in the old snowpack, sometimes consisting of buried surface hoar, are unfavourable. Especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes the avalanches can be triggered in the weakly bonded old snow and reach medium size. This applies in particular in case of a large load.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 5 to 20 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1700 m.

As a consequence of a strong northwesterly wind, hard wind slabs formed since Monday in particular in areas not adjacent to ridgelines.
Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was good. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day, in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.

Adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. Below the wind-eroded peaks, above around 1600 m, more snow is lying for the time of year.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on steep slopes individual small to medium-sized slab avalanches are possible above approximately 1400 m.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs and can be released easily.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The high temperatures from the late morning will give rise to increasing softening of the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a storm force wind from northerly directions, clearly visible wind slabs formed in all aspects. These can be released even by a single winter sport participant above approximately 2200 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized. Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The gusty wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released by large loads in particular.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The snowpack will be well bonded at low and intermediate altitudes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from northeasterly directions, clearly visible wind slabs formed in the last few days in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. These can be released, in particular by large loads and reach medium size.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the icy crust.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Since Monday the wind has been strong over a wide area. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In particular sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

In shady places that are protected from the wind high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The upper section of the snowpack is dry and its surface has a crust that is not capable of bearing a load. In very isolated cases weak layers exist in the top section of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of natural moist avalanches being released will increase a little on very steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant above the tree line, caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow are to be assessed with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a strong to storm force northerly wind, large surface-area wind slabs formed in the last few days. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on steep slopes dry slab avalanches are possible. Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads. This applies in particular above the tree line, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack on very steep shady slopes. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in east to north to west facing aspects. Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are possible especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported some snow. As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, sometimes large wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The fresh wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on east to north to west facing aspects and generally above the tree line.
The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to softening of the snowpack below approximately 2000 m.

tendency.highlights: Friday: The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: In particular on steep slopes more mostly small avalanches are possible as a consequence of the ceasing of precipitation.

snowpackStructure.comment: New snow and wind slabs are lying on a moist old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above the tree line. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment: The wind will be moderate to strong. The wind has transported some snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks a generally favourable avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs are mostly small and can be released by large loads at their margins in particular.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1500 m small small avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be generally stable. The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
